lines.speaker: Produced by
lines.text: Robert Carlock and Wendy Knoller
Central Perk

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (she enters) Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Ross, Rachel and Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Honey, I got us that room at the Woodford Inn this weekend.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That place in Vermont? You can take a hint!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait, you can't go away this weekend! It's Emma's birthday!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We're having a party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, can't you just have the party when we get back?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, that day... that won't be her real birthday!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Gee if only she were one and had no idea what the hell a birthday was!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: C'mon you guys, this is really important to us.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I'm sorry, but Chandler and I could really use a weekend away. You know, to reconnect... emotionally.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's this thing I really want us to do. I read about it in Maxim...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, can't you just go to Vermont the next day?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, we want everyone to be there. As much as I hate to delay your doing weird sex stuff to my little sister.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: And I mean, you know, you guys... This is a big deal. I mean, how can we have her first birthday party without her aunt and her uncle!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, we'll stay. We can just drive up after the party.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine, but if we end up not doing this Maxim thing because of this party...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Believe me, that is not why we won't be doing that!
OPENING CREDITS
Rachel and Joey's apartment

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You know Pheebs, when I was little, on my birthday, my daddy would hide a present in every room of the house, and then he would draw a treasure map to help me find 'em all.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oooh, I love family traditions like that. When uhm... when Ursula and I were kids, on our birthday, our stepdad would sell his blood to buy us food!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (he enters) Hey guys!

lines.speaker: Rachel and Phoebe
lines.text: Hi!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, I brought the camera for Emma's video.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, good, good! We had this idea to make a birthday video for Emma and we'll give it to her when she is 18.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, COOL!! Wow, it's like a time capsule!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, just think... she's gonna be watching that video on a TV that hasn't even been invented yet! With friends who right now are just like babies! And they'll be living in a floating city that the humans built to escape the ant people!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's the hope! So, is Emma awake yet?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no, it's still nap time. But she'll be up soon.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, and where is Joey?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I said it's still nap time.
Joey comes out from his room, yawning

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (taping Joey with the cam) Hey, there is uncle Joey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, say something to Emma on her 18th birthday!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 18, uh? (starts to prepare himself to say his "How You Doin'" line)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, NO!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What, what!? it's for her hot friends!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: When they see this you'll be 52!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: And starting to think about settling down!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey Joey, will you please set this up for people to put Emma's presents on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'd love to! Yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (To Phoebe) We were supposed to bring presents?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah! I wrote Emma a song.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, yeah! How was I supposed to know?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey, it's a birthday party.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, but for a one-year-old. What's the point... the other day she laughed for like an hour at a cup. Just a cup with a picture of Elmo on it dressed as a farmer. And he's standing next to this cow and the cow says "El-moo"! (Joey starts laughing) Yeah... that's a funny cup!
Monica and Chandler enter

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey hey! Where's the birthday girl?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, she's still napping

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, sure, she was probably up all night, excited about the party she knows is happening.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, I know that you guys really want to get to Vermont and this isn't a really big deal to you, but it really is to us, ok? Emma will never have a first birthday again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right... you're right. We're sorry. Now let's wake up Emma and get the fun time started!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No really, she didn't sleep well last night, so we can't wake her up.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you freaking kidding me, Green?
someone knocks on the door. Rachel goes to open it

lines.speaker: Jack and Judy
lines.text: HI!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (points the camcorder towards them to record their entrance) Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So glad you came!

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: I can't believe Emma is already one!

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: (to Monica) I remember your first birthday! Ross was jealous of all the attention we were giving you. He pulled on his testicles so hard! We had to take him to the emergency room!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pointing the camcorder at himself) There's something you didn't know about your dad!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Mr. and Mrs. Geller! Let me help you with that.

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: Thank you!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh man, this is great, uh? The three of us together again! You know what would be fun? If we gave this present to Emma from all of us!

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: Which one are you?
Time lapse

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe Emma is still asleep!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know, what are we gonna do?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I've got a plan. I've got a plan. I'm going to ram this platter really hard into your ribs. You're gonna scream out and that'll wake her up!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not going to Vermont with this Monica!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Pheebs, you know what? I was thinking... since you wrote a song, maybe I could do something for Emma using my talents!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: So you're gonna... hit on her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no, no! My talents as an actor!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You know, I could like maybe... I could do a dramatic reading of one of her books!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Or you could stick a fork in an apple!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I think Emma might like it!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Emma might like what?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Um, my present!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What did you get her?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Actually we prepared performances.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Separate performances.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But equally real!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, this sounds like fun! Well, you know what? Actually? People are getting a little antsy waiting Emma to wake up from her nap, so would you mind performing them once now?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sure, yeah!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (unconfidently) O K.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to everybody) All right, let's get this party started, huh? Joey and Phoebe are gonna perform a little something for us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, great!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (to Joey) Are you gonna be embarrassed going up there having nothing prepared?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I do it every week with three cameras pointed at me and a whole crew waiting!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, Joey, what are you gonna do for us?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I will be doing a dramatic reading of one of Emma's books.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, ok, which one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, why, it's a... (he picks a book up) one of her favorites, uh, (he reads the title of the book) "Riding the Storm Out. Coping with post-partum depression" eesh! (he puts the book back and picks up another) "Love you forever". Love you forever. By Robert Munsch. Published by Firefly books. Printed (he pauses and changes the tone to a dramatic one) in Mexico. A mother held her new baby and very slowly rocked him back and forth, back and forth, back and forth, and while she held him she sang "I'll love you forever, I'll like you for always, as long as I'm living, my baby you'll be". (the picture fades and Joey is now finishing the book). And while he rocked her, he sang "I'll love you forever, I'll like you for always, as long as I'm living, my baby you'll be".

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (crying) Wow! That was amazing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thank you so much for that gift!
Chandler (nearly weeping): I was not ready for this today!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Amazing... amazing.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Phoebe, I'm sorry! Phoebe has prepared something as well.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's right, I've prepared a song for Emma. From my heart to hers. For there's no greater gift, than the gift of music. (she starts singing) Emma! Your name poses a dilemma. 'Cause not much else rhymes with Emma! Maybe the actor Richard Crenna, he played the commanding officer in Rambo. Happy birthday Emma!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Is that it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, of course not! I also, you know, prepared a reading (she picks up a book). "Sex and the single mother. (pause) Finding your G-spot.

lines.speaker: Everybody
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no!
Joey's Apartment. Ross is filming his parents

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Hello Emma. Happy eighteenth birthday.

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: Right now that seems so far away, seventeen years.

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Yes, you'll be all grown up by then. We'll be... Well your grandfather and I might not be here.

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: That's true! This message could becoming to you from beyond the grave, Emma!

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: After all, my parents died very young.

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: And my cholesterol's off the charts!

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Remember, Emma, heart disease kills women too!
Ross (stops recording): Ok, cut! Great. That was... that was just... yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, um, don't forget to get a shot of Emma's cake. It's in a box in the fridge.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sure.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you're gonna love this cake. I got it from a bakery in New Jersey, Corino's.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, that place has the creamiest frosting! I use to hitchhike there when I was a kid.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, anyway, they make these great novelty cakes, in all different shapes, and if you give them a photo, they'll copy it in icing!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, did you do a picture of Emma?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! On a cake shaped like a bunny.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, Rach? Does this bakery by any chance also bake erotic cakes? Say for bachelorette parties?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross, what are you talking about? (she sees the cake) oh! Oh my God! They put my baby's face on a penis!
Phoebe (sees the cake): oh! Now it's a party!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Why you guys this isn't funny, all right? If I wanted this cake to be a disaster I would have baked it myself!
Joey (staring at the cake): Uh.. is it ok that I still think it looks delicious?
Judy:(to Jack) Jack, look at this.

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: I know what you're thinking Judy, the resemblance is uncanny!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am this close to tugging on my testicles again.
Rachel (on the phone): No, no, this is not what I ordered. Ok? I went all the way to New Jersey so that I could have the perfect cake for my daughter's birthday and I need a bunny cake, right now!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ask them if it would be faster if we cut the baby's face off the penis, so we can put it on the bunny. (pause). That is a weird sentence!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Believe you me! I am going to bring this cake back, I don't even want it in my home... (Turns towards the cake and sees Joey trying to take a piece and yells at him) Joey, don't touch it!!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm so confused!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (speaking to the person on the phone again) Yes, yes. I still want my daughters picture, but on a bunny cake. Yellow cake, chocolate frosting with nuts!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To be fair this one does have nuts.
Time lapse
Ross, Chandler and Monica are in the kitchen area

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey Mon, that was really nice of you to loan Rachel your car so she could go and get the cake.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. So nice of her to pull my hair, 'till I dropped the key!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, you know what? While we're waiting, you guys could tape your message to Emma for her 18th birthday, huh? (takes the camcorder and points it at Monica and Chandler) Ok!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi Emma! It's the year 2020. Are you still enjoying your nap?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're Aunt Monica and uncle Chandler, by the way. You may not recognize us, because we haven't spoken to your parents in seventeen years!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We used to be married, but then we missed a weekend away together and things kind of unraveled. Because of you! Happy Birthday. (Ross looks disappointed and switches the camcorder off)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, Rachel promised it would be over by now. We seriously have to go, if we want to get to Vermont. I called them and the last train leaves in a half hour.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: And you know, I have a massage client soon.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You guys, just please.. a little bit longer. I promise, Rachel will be back with the cake any minute. Monica, remember.. the frosting? huh?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Alright, 5 more minutes.
The phone rings, Ross picks up

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (Into receiver) Hello? (listens) Oh no! What happened? (listens some more) Ok ok, where are you? (Grabs a pen and starts writing). Ok, I'll be right there. (Puts the phone down)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Was that Emma? Is she up?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, Rachel got pulled over for speeding. She forgot her licence so now I have to bring it to her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, if you're leaving, I'm definitely gonna go.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! Wait! If anybody gets to go.. it's us (Points at herself and Chandler) We've been complaining the longest!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, wait ! You guys, no, no, you can't leave! Rachel already feels bad that the cake's messed up. How do you think she's gonna feel when she comes back here and all you guys are gone?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know! You'll tell us on Monday!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey! You're in charge, ok? You make sure nobody leaves! (leaves)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Got it! (Goes towards the front door and stands with his back to it) Hey hey hey! (to Chandler) Where do you think you're going?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: To the bathroom!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Alright, well the rest of you get comfortable, ok, because we're gonna be here for a li...(stops and thinks) Wait a minute, there is a window in there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no! He's not getting away that easy! (Phoebe and Joey run towards the bathroom and enter)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Yelling) What are you doing? Get the hell out of here! (Phoebe and Joey come out looking shocked)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, that one did not have Emma's face on it.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it did not.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No.

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Well, we better get going, it's late. Jack's not allowed to drive at night anymore. He has trouble staying in his lane.

lines.speaker: Jack
lines.text: Last winter I went up on a church lawn and drove right through a manger scene. The papers thought it was a hate crime.

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Anyway, it was lovely seeing you.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok! (Opens the door for them)

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Bye... Bye dear.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nighty-night!

lines.speaker: Judy
lines.text: Nighty-night!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Bye, bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey? How could you just let them leave?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, hey, hey, hey! I'm not gonna mess with Jack, he's a great man, he fought for our country.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No, he didn't! He pretended to be a Quaker to get out of Korea.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Shocked) Oh! Well that's it! He's the last one to go. I'm locking you guys in. (turns the bolts of the door, thereby locking it)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You do know, I can just turn them the other way around, right?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! I forgot you used to live here!
The phone rings, Joey picks up

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: (on the other side of the line) Joey! It's Estelle!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! (smiles)

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: So, how did your audition go today?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (smile fades) What audition?

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: The one I told you about last week?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What? You never said anything about an audition!

lines.speaker: Estelle
lines.text: (Looks confused) Let me start over. I just got a call about an audition. I think you can still make it. It's down at the Astor Theatre and you need to have a monologue prepared.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: A monologue? I don't have.. (sees the book he was reading before for his "dramatic reading") I got it. (hangs up) (announces to the room) Aah! so... I'm gonna take off!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wh.. what? No wait, you don't get to leave! I've got a massage client waiting outside my door any minute!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, and we've paid for a room, that we're supposed to be in right now!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, oh, ok now... Only one of us has to stay with Emma. Ok, and as the person who realized that, I get to go!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! no! Let's figure out a fair way to decide who's staying.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh! I got it! Ok, everyone pick a number from one to ten. Alright? Whoever gets the highest number gets to go first.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (She gives Joey a you-are-so-stupid-look) Ok, ten.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, Monica picks ten, I call nine! Anyone else?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, lets just draw straws.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or.. we could flip a coin, and then multiply the..

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm begging you stop.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Right.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ok, how about this: We got wind up toys for Emma for her birthday. We can make them race, and whoever comes in last, stays!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Visibly excited) Yeah!! Let's do that!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Also very excited) That sounds more fun than the thing we were going to do in Vermont!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Everybody get your toys! (They all run toward the table with toys)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok! I want the dolphin!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Thats a bear.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm too excited!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, you get the bear, uhm, Joey, you get the robot, and Chandler and I get the dog. Ok, and the race is going to go (Takes two cups and marks the start and finish lines with them) from here to here. Now the one who comes in last, stays!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, ladies and gentlemen, wind your toys! (they do so)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, on your mark... Get set... GO!!!
Monica, Phoebe and Joey release their wind-up toys.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Go! Go!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Come on robot!
Phoebe's bear takes the lead, followed by Joey's robot, and far behind is Chandler and Monica's dog, which walks a few paces, stops and starts barking, sits, walks again, and so on...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (to the dog) What are you barking at?
While Phoebe's bear is still in the lead, it is now closely followed by Joey's robot. Chandler and Monica's dog however, sits down, barking... and does a backflip.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wha... How the hell is that gonna help?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I bought you. How did I forget that that's all you do?
Meanwhile the race has been won by Phoebe's bear, followed by Joey's robot

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Way to go robot!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good job Alan!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, good race you guys.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, see you later!
Joey and Phoebe leave for the door

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No! No, no... wait! We didn't lose. (turns to Chandler) The rules clearly stated that the last one to cross the finish line was the loser. Well, our dog never crossed the finish line, so technically...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They left.
Monica turns to see the closed door.
The freeway. Rachel is driving Monica's Porsche, while using her mobile phone. Ross is sitting next to her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (into the phone) No, there isn't time to go to the bakery. We're just gonna come home... Everybody left? Alright, well just tell Emma we're gonna be there as soon as we can. (emotionally) Bye...

She hangs up, closes her phone, turns around and puts it in her bag which is in the back of the car. While doing this and not looking at the road, she turns the steering wheel by accident, which makes the car swerve.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: RACHEL!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: OOH! God! Sorry!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (sarcastically) I can't believe they gave you a ticket. You're such a good driver.
Ross fastens his seat belt.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Emma's awake.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I can't believe this. This is her first birthday. She's awake. We're not even there. Everybody left. We still have this stupid obscene cake.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, maybe I can fix that, you know. Try to turn it into something else. (he opens the box)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, why do you even bother? I already ruined her first birthday... And do you know how important these early experiences are Ross? Very! According to the back cover of that book that you gave me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach, she's not going to remember this.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (shakes her head) I guess... Oh, I just had such an idea of what this day would be like, you know? Emma laughing and everybody gathered around her cake singing "Happy Birthday". Then we would all go into... HEY GET OUT OF THE ROAD YOU STUPID STUDENT DRIVER!!! (honks furiously, and Ross looks at her in disbelief and Rachel looks at him.) They have to learn!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looks back at the cake) Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's not bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she looks at the cake) Oh my God! Look what... you made it into a bunny. How did you do that?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, I just made these two things uhm... cheeks. And then I split this to make ears.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I'm very impressed.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Some can sing, some can dance. I apparently can turn phallic cakes into woodland creatures.
Rachel and Joey's apartment. Chandler, Monica and Emma are sitting on the floor, and Emma's stuffed animals are lined up in front of them.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now another way to organise your stuffed animals, is by size.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm sorry, is this a game for Emma or for Monica?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Game?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Emma, you even know it's your birthday today? You're one! One-year-old, that's little.
With his index finger he shows her how old she is. Emma also points her finger and babbles...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ooh! That's my girl!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's how old you are.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Did I teach her that? Did I just... impart wisdom?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (embraces Emma tightly) Ooh, I want one...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me too...
There's a couple of seconds of silence. Then Chandler looks around...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: There's no-one around. Why don't we just take this one?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: ...and head to Canada!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I was kidding.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I wasn't. Let's get going!
Phoebe enters the apartment again.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey, are Ross and Rachel back?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, not yet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh good, I didn't miss the party.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about your massage client?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I just felt so bad, missing this. So I just slipped him a little something, you know. As long as I'm back in five or six hours, it will be alright.
Joey comes running into the apartment, out of breath.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, if Ross and Rachel ask, I've been here the whole time. THE WHOLE TIME!
Ross and Rachel enter.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (to Ross and Rachel) I've been here the whole time!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Joey, we just saw you come in. You ran past us on the stairs.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't care that you left. I'm just glad that you're here. Thanks you guys!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, guys! Come on! You gotta see what Emma just did.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Emma, how old are you? How old are you today? (holds up his index finger again)

lines.speaker: Emma
lines.text: Wo-ish. (and holds up her finger)
Ross and Rachel gasp

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh! Emma, that's right! You're that many!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh my God! Our daughter's a genius! Rach, this means...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (angrily) NO, no science camp!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damnit! I'll put a candle on the cake.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh!... Oh and Emma, look at your stuffed animals lined up so neatly!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, here we go! Emma's first birthday cake... Well hey... well, blow out the candle. Come on Emma.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let's do it, come on!
Rachel wipes away some tears.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's wrong? Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah, nothing! These are happy tears! This is just what I wanted.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (pointing at cake) Hey, you made it into a bunny.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is wrong with me. It looked more delicious when it was a penis.
ENDING CREDITS
Rachel and Joey's apartment. It's just Ross and Rachel. They put the camera on a tripod in the kitchen and they are standing in front of the camera.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, you ready?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A-a-and... record.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. (they both look into the camera, and Ross waves.) Hi Emma. Well, your first birthday is over, and it was really...

There's a lot of yelling and screaming coming from the hallway, and they get up to look at what's the noise all about. In the hallway, Monica, Chandler, Joey and Phoebe are having another wind-up animal race, yelling and screaming fanatically.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (yelling at the top of her voice) Go, go, go Alan! Run you, hairy bastard!
End
